sections.paragraph: an unused root, whence are derived לָבִיא, לְבִי, לְבִיָּא, a lion, a lioness. I have hardly any doubt of its being onomatopoetic, in imitation of the sound of roaring; like the old Germ. luwen, lüven, leuen; Engl. to low; whence the Germ. Löwe, Leu; Gr. λέων. [In Thes. another origin is also suggested; Arab. لبى to be voracious.]
